sanskrit:
आहुस्त्वामृषयः सर्वे देवर्षिर्नारदस्तथा।
असितो देवलो व्यासः स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे॥
transliteration: āhus tvām ṛṣayaḥ sarve devarṣir nāradas tathā | asito devalo vyāsaḥ svayaṁ caiva bravīṣi me ||
literal_meaning: All the sages say of You, as do the divine-sage Nārada, Asita, Devala, Vyāsa, and You Yourself now tell me.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s cognitive map has shifted from doubt to radical trust; he now externalizes a consensus reality in which Krishna is the supreme organizing principle of psyche and cosmos.
psychological_state: Post-revelation consolidation: the psyche stabilizes around a new core narrative, reducing cognitive dissonance by anchoring to respected external validators (sages) and internal confirmation (Krishna’s own words).
trigger_scenario: After months of therapy, a client hears the same insight from three trusted mentors and then from their own inner voice—suddenly the new self-story feels objectively true.
anchor_text: Trust the wise who all point to one inner guide.
